ucs.title: [LLM] MuddyWater (Mango Sandstorm) RMM staging — first-seen ScreenConnect/Atera/SimpleHelp/RemoteUtilities client on a host
ucs.description: Hunts MuddyWater's signature 2023-2026 tradecraft: phishing emails delivering MSI/HTA loaders that install a legitimate commercial RMM agent (ScreenConnect/ConnectWise Control, Atera, SimpleHelp, RemoteUtilities, Syncro, AnyDesk) for hands-on-keyboard C2 via vendor cloud infra. Anti-baselined against 30d of legitimate IT-managed RMM use to surface only the first-time deployment per host.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=false count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdlines values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parents values(Processes.process_hash) as hashes from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where earliest=-24h Processes.process_name IN ("ScreenConnect.ClientService.exe","ScreenConnect.WindowsClient.exe","ConnectWiseControl.ClientService.exe","rutserv.exe","rfusclient.exe","AteraAgent.exe","atera_agent.exe","Syncro.Service.exe","SyncroMSP.Installer.exe","AnyDesk.exe","SimpleHelp.exe","SimpleHelpCustomer.exe","sra.exe","RemoteAccess.exe","goto-opener.exe","LogMeIn.exe") Processes.user!="*$" by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=outer dest process_name [ | tstats summariesonly=false count as baseline_count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where earliest=-30d@d latest=-24h@h Processes.process_name IN ("ScreenConnect.ClientService.exe","ScreenConnect.WindowsClient.exe","ConnectWiseControl.ClientService.exe","rutserv.exe","rfusclient.exe","AteraAgent.exe","atera_agent.exe","Syncro.Service.exe","SyncroMSP.Installer.exe","AnyDesk.exe","SimpleHelp.exe","SimpleHelpCustomer.exe","sra.exe","RemoteAccess.exe","goto-opener.exe","LogMeIn.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` ] | where isnull(baseline_count) | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | table firstTime lastTime dest user process_name cmdlines parents hashes
ucs.kql:
let RmmBinaries = dynamic([
  "screenconnect.clientservice.exe","screenconnect.windowsclient.exe",
  "connectwisecontrol.clientservice.exe",
  "rutserv.exe","rfusclient.exe",
  "ateraagent.exe","atera_agent.exe",
  "syncro.service.exe","syncromsp.installer.exe",
  "anydesk.exe","simplehelp.exe","simplehelpcustomer.exe",
  "sra.exe","remoteaccess.exe",
  "goto-opener.exe","logmein.exe"
]);
let BaselineDays = 30d;
let RecentHours = 24h;
let RmmBaseline = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp between (ago(BaselineDays) .. ago(RecentHours))
    | where FileName in~ (RmmBinaries)
    | summarize by DeviceId, FileName;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(RecentHours)
| where FileName in~ (RmmBinaries)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| join kind=leftanti RmmBaseline on DeviceId, FileName
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          RmmTool = FileName, RmmPath = FolderPath,
          RmmCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
          ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd  = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          GrandParent = InitiatingProcessParentFileName,
          SHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let RmmExes = dynamic([
  "screenconnect.clientservice.exe","screenconnect.windowsclient.exe",
  "connectwisecontrol.clientservice.exe",
  "rutserv.exe","rfusclient.exe",
  "ateraagent.exe","atera_agent.exe",
  "syncro.service.exe","syncromsp.installer.exe",
  "anydesk.exe","simplehelp.exe","simplehelpcustomer.exe",
  "sra.exe","remoteaccess.exe",
  "goto-opener.exe","logmein.exe"
]);
let BaselineDays = 30d;
let RecentHours = 24h;
let Baseline = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated between (ago(BaselineDays) .. ago(RecentHours))
    | where EventID == 4688
    | extend Exe = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
    | where Exe in (RmmExes)
    | summarize by Computer, Exe;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(RecentHours)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| extend Exe = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| where Exe in (RmmExes)
| join kind=leftanti Baseline on Computer, Exe
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
          RmmTool = Exe, NewProcessName, CommandLine,
          ParentProcessName, TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.rationale: MuddyWater abandoned bespoke implants in favour of commercial RMM agents around 2022 (Microsoft, Sentinel ONE and Group-IB all confirm). The current playbook — Mango Sandstorm 2023-2024, MuddyWater/TA450 2024-2026 — is a phishing PDF/HTML linking to file-share storage hosting an MSI that installs ScreenConnect, Atera, SimpleHelp or RemoteUtilities directly. The agent then phones home to legitimate

ucs.title: [LLM] MuddyWater PowGoop loader — goopdate.dll side-loaded by GoogleUpdate.exe from non-Google path
ucs.description: PowGoop is MuddyWater's signature loader for the POWERSTATS/PHONYC2 framework. It drops a legitimate signed GoogleUpdate.exe alongside a malicious goopdate.dll into a writable directory (%APPDATA%, %TEMP%, %PROGRAMDATA%, user profile). Detection anchors on the canonical signature: GoogleUpdate.exe loading goopdate.dll from anywhere except the real Google install root.
ucs.splunk:
`sysmon` EventCode=7 ImageLoaded="*\\goopdate.dll" Image="*\\GoogleUpdate.exe" NOT (Image="C:\\Program Files\\Google\\*" OR Image="C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Google\\*")
| stats min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime count values(Hashes) as hashes values(Signed) as signed_status by host User Image ImageLoaded
| convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
| table firstTime lastTime host User Image ImageLoaded hashes signed_status count
ucs.kql:
DeviceImageLoadEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where FileName =~ "goopdate.dll"
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "GoogleUpdate.exe"
| where not(InitiatingProcessFolderPath startswith @"C:\Program Files\Google\")
   and not(InitiatingProcessFolderPath startswith @"C:\Program Files (x86)\Google\")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName,
          LoadedDll        = FileName,
          LoadedDllPath    = FolderPath,
          LoadedDllSha256  = SHA256,
          Loader           = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          LoaderPath       = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          LoaderCmd        = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          LoaderSha256     = InitiatingProcessSHA256,
          User             = InitiatingProcessAccountName
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
WindowsEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where Provider == "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon"
| where EventID == 7
| extend Image        = tostring(EventData.Image),
         ImageLoaded  = tostring(EventData.ImageLoaded),
         User         = tostring(EventData.User),
         Hashes       = tostring(EventData.Hashes),
         Signed       = tostring(EventData.Signed)
| where ImageLoaded endswith @"\goopdate.dll"
| where Image endswith @"\GoogleUpdate.exe"
| where not(Image startswith @"C:\Program Files\Google\")
   and not(Image startswith @"C:\Program Files (x86)\Google\")
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, User, Image, ImageLoaded, Hashes, Signed
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: MuddyWater PowGoop Loader - goopdate.dll Side-Loaded by GoogleUpdate.exe Outside Google Install Path
id: 7f3a2c4b-9d18-4c2a-a7e6-3b8f1d5c2e9a
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects MuddyWater (G0069 / Mango Sandstorm / MERCURY / Static Kitten) PowGoop
  loader. PowGoop drops a legitimate signed GoogleUpdate.exe with a malicious
  goopdate.dll into a writable directory; the signed binary then side-loads the
  attacker DLL. Legitimate goopdate.dll only loads from the Google install root.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0069/
  - https://research.checkpoint.com/2022/iranian-apt-muddywater-pursues-israeli-targets/
  - https://www.mandiant.com/resources/blog/telegram-malware-iranian-espionage
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/11
tags:
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.t1574.002
  - attack.g0069
logsource:
  product: windows
  category: image_load
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\GoogleUpdate.exe'
    ImageLoaded|endswith: '\goopdate.dll'
  filter_legit_path:
    Image|startswith:
      - 'C:\Program Files\Google\'
      - 'C:\Program Files (x86)\Google\'
  condition: selection and not filter_legit_path
falsepositives:
  - Corrupted Google Updater install relocated by the user (extremely rare)
level: high
ucs.rationale: PowGoop is a MuddyWater-exclusive loader publicly tied to G0069 by Mandiant, Check Point, Deep Instinct, and the US/UK CISA advisory AA22-055A. The DLL name 'goopdate.dll' is the legitimate Google Update helper DLL; the technique mimics signed-binary side-loading. Because the real GoogleUpdate.exe only ever loads goopdate.dll from C:\Program Files\Google\Update\ (or the x86 equivalent), any other
